source.title: Behavioral variability through stochastic choice and its gating by anterior cingulate cortex

confidence_rationale: Direct circuit-level test of how LC-NE input and ACC output jointly control behavioural variability in rats. Transgenic perturbation isolates LC -> ACC as the causal pathway for strategic-vs-stochastic mode switching. The paper is the load-bearing empirical anchor for Q-045: it argues against full independence of the LC-NE noise substrate (MECH-313) and the ACC anti-recency substrate (MECH-260), because LC-NE acts THROUGH ACC to suppress history-based decisions. Direction tagged 'mixed' because Q-045 framed the two as parallel substrates; this paper says LC drives, ACC mediates -- partial collapse at the circuit level, not full independence. Confidence high (0.84): in vivo causal perturbation in transgenic rats, peer-reviewed in Cell, direct relevance to the substrate-coupling question.
failure_signatures: If REE treats MECH-313 (LC-NE tonic noise floor) and MECH-260 (dACC anti-recency) as fully orthogonal axes with independent control, the 4-arm ablation will show 313-only and 260-only producing comparable behavioural diversity. Tervo predicts an ASYMMETRY: lesioning ACC (analogue to MECH-260 OFF) leaves the LC-driven stochastic-mode-switch upstream signal intact but with no downstream effector, so the agent should fail to enter stochastic mode under unbeatable-competitor conditions. Lesioning LC-NE (analogue to MECH-313 OFF) removes the trigger but leaves ACC's normal history-tracking intact, so the agent should over-rely on history-based strategy and likewise fail to enter stochastic mode -- but for a different reason. Same end-state behaviour by two different mechanisms.

If REE's MECH-313 implements a state-INDEPENDENT noise floor (always-on), and MECH-260 implements a state-DEPENDENT recency penalty (only fires when recency is detected), the 4-arm dissociation will work as designed. But if both are implemented as 'just add gaussian noise on the policy logits with different temporal kernels', they will collapse functionally even though they are architecturally distinct -- the falsifier will fail to dissociate them and produce both-OFF / 313-only / 260-only / both-ON rankings that are statistically indistinguishable from each other.

If REE has no analogue of the LC -> ACC directional input (i.e. MECH-313 and MECH-260 are not wired together at the circuit level), the 4-arm experiment will fail to reproduce Tervo's central finding: in their data, ACC normal engagement is SUPPRESSED during stochastic mode, suggesting that LC-NE not only enables variability but actively gates ACC offline. REE should predict the same -- both-ON should look behaviourally MORE like 313-only than like 260-only, because LC dominance suppresses ACC contribution in the stochastic regime.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Rats engage two distinct behavioural modes when challenged with a virtual competitor: a STRATEGIC mode in which history-based and model-based predictions guide choice, and a STOCHASTIC mode in which outcomes associated with the rat's own actions are ignored and choices become unpredictable. The stochastic mode is engaged specifically when the competitor cannot be defeated by counter-prediction. Switching between modes is controlled by noradrenergic input from locus coeruleus into anterior cingulate cortex: optogenetic perturbation of this pathway changes the mode-switching threshold. During stochastic mode, normal ACC engagement (its history-tracking output) is SUPPRESSED. The authors interpret this as NE preventing erroneous beliefs (over-reliance on inferred competitor strategy) from guiding decisions when those beliefs cannot help.
mapping.ree_translation: Tervo 2014 anchors the Q-045 verdict directly. It shows that the LC-NE 'tonic-noise-floor' substrate (MECH-313 analogue) and the ACC 'history-tracking / anti-recency' substrate (MECH-260 analogue) are NOT independent parallel mechanisms: they are coupled at the circuit level, with LC-NE upstream of ACC and the LC signal acting to SUPPRESS the ACC output during stochastic mode. For REE this means: (a) the 4-arm ablation should not be expected to produce a clean orthogonal factorial; both-ON should look more like 313-only than like 260-only because LC dominance over-rides ACC; (b) the architectural commitment is to keep MECH-313 and MECH-260 as separate sub-mechanisms (they ARE substrate-distinct), but to acknowledge directional coupling -- MECH-313 should be able to suppress MECH-260's contribution when active; (c) the 'collapse into one substrate' resolution path in the Q-045 notes is partially correct (they are functionally coupled) but the correct framing is COUPLED-NOT-COLLAPSED: distinct substrates with directional gating, not a single combined regulator.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Rat data on a virtual-competitor task; the mapping to REE's reef-foraging substrate (SD-054) and gated-policy architecture (ARC-062) is by analogy. Tervo's stochastic mode is a discrete state-switch triggered by unbeatable-opponent detection; REE's MECH-313 is conceived as a continuous always-on noise floor and MECH-313 was not designed to be state-switched. The mapping is to the underlying neuromodulatory architecture (LC -> ACC directional coupling), not to the surface behaviour (mode-switching vs continuous noise). If REE wants the strongest validation, it would need a state-switch analogue (e.g. an inferred-unbeatability signal that gates MECH-313 amplitude). Q-045 as written does not include this; the lit-pull surfaces it as a follow-on architectural question. According to PubMed, DOI 10.1016/j.cell.2014.08.037; PMID 25259917.
mapping.source_context: Rat behaviour + transgenic optogenetic circuit perturbation, Cell 2014. Virtual-competitor task: rat plays an opponent that is either beatable by counter-prediction (strategic mode) or unbeatable by any strategy (stochastic mode). LC-cre transgenic line + viral targeting of LC -> ACC projection. Behavioural read-out: choice-history entropy + win-stay/lose-shift balance.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality very high (Cell 2014, transgenic causal perturbation, behavioural + circuit-level evidence). Mapping fidelity strong on the question of LC <-> ACC coupling (the central Q-045 axis), moderate on the question of state-switch vs continuous regulation -- the paper is a state-switch finding and Q-045's MECH-313 is continuous, so the mapping is at the architectural level, not the dynamic level. Transfer risk low: rat-to-REE for circuit-level architecture is a well-trodden analogy for the cingulate cortex; the LC -> ACC directional coupling is conserved across mammalian species per Aston-Jones & Cohen 2005 (already in arc_065 file).
